A note on deploying Express to Vercel — the steps and the gotchas.
The Express project here is scaffolded with Express Generator:
language-bash shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
So the structure looks like this:
. Open the Vercel site and sign in — I'm using GitHub.
I've used Vercel before, so I click New Project in the top right. If it's your first time, you'll see this screen:
Search for your project name and click Import.
Fill in the Project Name and Environment Variables as needed. We don't need env vars here, so just click Deploy.
You'll notice Framework Preset doesn't have Express or Node.js — that's normal.
Deploy is really fast. A moment later you'll land on this screen:
Now go to the project console — there's a Visit button. Click it and you'll see:
So we successfully deployed Express to Vercel, but there's more to do — otherwise you'll keep hitting
So how do we fix it? I found
, but it doesn't quite match our case — that guide is for APIs, and our project also serves pages.
But it does mention something important — the
file, used to configure Vercel. We can use that to solve our problem.
language-json shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
Deploy with this file in place, and the page renders correctly.
Vercel auto-detects commits, so you don't need to manually redeploy.
And the API that couldn't be hit before now works too:
For more on this config file, see
